fundamental_rights: Article 21 — Right to Life (includes right to health and safe products)
Article 19(1)(g) — Right to trade (balanced with consumer protection)
Article 14 — Right to Equality (consumers treated fairly)

laws.name: Consumer Protection Act, 2019
sections: Section 35 (complaint to District Commission — up to Rs 1 crore)
Section 47 (State Commission — Rs 1 crore to Rs 10 crore)
laws.summary: Provides three-tier dispute resolution: District, State, and National Consumer Commission. Covers defective goods, deficient services, unfair trade practices, and product liability.
laws.remedy: File complaint at e-Daakhil portal (edaakhil.nic.in) — online and free

laws.name: Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, 2016 (RERA)
sections: Section 18 (return of amount + interest for delays)
laws.summary: Protects homebuyers from builder fraud, delays, and false promises.
laws.remedy: File complaint at your state's RERA website

laws.name: Food Safety and Standards Act, 2006 (FSSAI)
laws.summary: Covers food quality, adulteration, and safety standards.
laws.remedy: Complain to FSSAI at fssai.gov.in or local Food Safety Officer

laws.name: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority Act, 1999
laws.summary: Insurance companies must settle claims within 30 days. Rejection must be in writing.
laws.remedy: File complaint with IRDAI at igms.irda.gov.in or Insurance Ombudsman

laws.name: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) / IPC
laws.summary: Criminal remedies for fraud and cheating by sellers.
laws.remedy: File FIR at nearest police station

steps: Send a formal written complaint to the seller/service provider — give 30 days to respond
If no response or unsatisfactory reply: file online complaint at consumerhelpline.gov.in or call 1915
For amounts up to Rs 1 crore: file at District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission
Use e-Daakhil portal (edaakhil.nic.in) — filing fee is minimal (Rs 100-500)
For builder disputes: file at state RERA authority
For banking/insurance disputes: approach respective Ombudsman
For food safety: complain to local Food Safety Officer or FSSAI
Preserve: bill/invoice, product photos, all communications, bank statements, warranty card
